sections.paragraph:
ἐπανακάμπτω 1 aor. ἐπανέκαμψα (Aristot.; Aq., Sym., Theod. Is 35:10) to come back to a place or person, return (Syntipas p. 14, 8 al.) εἰς τὴν πόλιν Hs 1:2, 5. ἐπὶ τὰς παρθένους to the virgins Hs 9, 14, 1.—DELG s.v. κάμπτω.

aor. aor. = aorist

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Aq Aq , II a.d.—List 5

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

Syntipas Syntipas , a collection of fables under the name of Syntapis; before XI a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 358
